title: Origanum gussonei
intro: Origanum gussonei, also known as Italian oregano, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The health benefits of Origanum gussonei include its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include carvacrol, thymol, and rosmarinic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Origanum gussonei include essential oils, teas, and infusions, as well as capsules and tinctures that can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Origanum gussonei is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant belongs to the genus Origanum, which includes other popular herbs such as oregano and thyme. Origanum gussonei is closely related to other species within the same genus, sharing similar characteristics and growth habits. Further classification may include the plant's specific order, class, and kingdom within the plant taxonomy system.

parts: Origanum gussonei, commonly known as Greek oregano, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family.
